files.diff:
diff --git a/sim_notes.md b/sim_notes.md
index 5eb135e..6fee48a 100644
--- a/sim_notes.md
+++ b/sim_notes.md
@@ -68,2 +68,9 @@ Day 63 / Week 9 observations:
 - Usage peaked at 489k/day, still under tier 2, but continued growth could approach capacity.
 - Week 9 adjustment: keep proven ads, increase support spend, move to tier 3 capacity, and modestly raise A/B prices for new users.
+
+Day 70 / Week 10 observations:
+- Cash slipped only $11.5k while subscribers grew to 4,366, so the model is close to crossing into positive weekly cash flow.
+- S2 content marketing remains the best channel: 918 paid subscribers from 2,244 leads, with strong B/C uptake even after the price increase. S1 referral is still profitable but lower ARPU and more support-sensitive.
+- Open issues rose to 382, still same-day, while ops resolved 2,050 issues. Social posts still mention delayed support, so capacity must stay ahead of growth.
+- Competitor posts showed two quality boosts around day 69/70, raising the risk that current expectations drift faster than low global dev can offset.
+- Week 10 adjustment: hold prices and capacity, shift a little S1 referral spend into S2 content, raise S2-targeted support, and increase global/S2 dev to defend quality without starting unaffordable R&D.
